description_long: This is a traditional Japanese stone lantern, known as a tōrō, typically found in gardens, parks, or temple grounds. The lantern is constructed from a dark, speckled stone and features a broad, square base that tapers upward into a midsection supporting a roofed chamber. The chamber has open windows on each side, allowing light to emanate from within, and is topped by a gently sloping, pagoda-style roof with a small ornamental finial at the peak. The surfaces are angular and geometric, with clean lines and a robust, monolithic appearance. The stone texture is rough and weathered, giving the lantern an ancient, enduring quality. This lantern is designed to be both functional and decorative, serving as a source of light and a focal point in outdoor spaces.
description: A traditional Japanese stone lantern with a square base, open chamber, and pagoda-style roof, made from rough, dark stone. It features clean geometric lines and an ornamental finial on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square base, open chamber, and the full profile of the pagoda-style roof with the finial centered above.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the same features as the front, with subtle changes in shadow and perspective on the stone surfaces.
description_view_2: Another rotated view, nearly identical to the front, highlighting the symmetry and the open chamber's window.
description_view_3: Final view showing the lantern from the opposite side, maintaining the same architectural features and proportions.
